title: Sun's Galactic Orbit
body: The Sun orbits the center of the Milky Way galaxy once each $$2.6\times{10}^8$$ years, with a roughly circular orbit averaging a radius of $$3\times{10}^4$$ light-years. Take $$1$$ light-year $$=$$ $$9.461\times{10}^{15}$$ $$m$$ and $$1$$ year $$=$$ $$3.156\times{10}^7$$ s.
